2026-06-18 09:41:58

TurboRegime transitioned to RiskOff at the close of June 17 and is now one trading day into the new regime. The model currently holds a defensive allocation of 60% SPY / 40% SHV, reflecting a more cautious market outlook following recent weakness in the TReg Regime Signal.

While the broader market has remained relatively resilient, recent deterioration in relative performance caused the TReg signal to cross below its trigger threshold, resulting in the move from RiskOn to RiskOff. The objective of this regime is not to predict a bear market, but rather to reduce portfolio risk while the model evaluates whether recent weakness is temporary or the beginning of a more sustained pullback.

The new trade has started positively, generating a 0.32% gain on its first trading day with no meaningful drawdown. Because the regime change is fresh, the focus will be on monitoring whether the TReg Regime Signal stabilizes and begins to recover. A sustained improvement in signal strength would support a future return to RiskOn, while continued deterioration would reinforce the current defensive posture.

For now, market conditions favor maintaining a balanced, risk-aware allocation until additional evidence emerges regarding the market's next directional move
